executive_summary: Unit economics for VR elderly pets platform are deeply unfavorable — LTV/CAC ratio of 0.89x with 37-month payback driven by high hardware costs depressing gross margin to 55% and modest ARPU of $39/mo constrained by medium pain intensity and free alternatives.
score_reasoning: Score: 30/100 — LTV/CAC Ratio: 4/25 (LTV $715 / CAC $800 = 0.89x; hardware-enabled SaaS gross margin 55% from CloudZero/Benchmarkit 2025; churn 3% monthly from B2B SaaS SMB benchmarks; CAC $800 from eldercare tech benchmark) Revenue Model Validation: 13/25 (3 companies using subscription for senior living tech: Rendever 500+ communities with 95% retention, MyndVR $600K+ contracted revenue with multi-year deals, ElliQ government-subsidized distribution; Rendever reasonably well-documented) Payback Period: 3/25 (37.3 months; CAC $800 / monthly margin $21.45; well above 24-month concerning threshold) Pricing Power: 10/25 (pricing spans ~8x from $29.95-$250/mo; 1 free alternative Alcove; ElliQ at $59/mo as premium; narrow competitive band at low end constrains upward pricing) Total: 4 + 13 + 3 + 10 = 30

data.model_justification: All three direct competitors (Rendever, MyndVR, Alcove) use subscription models with hardware upfront + monthly/annual recurring fees. Senior living facilities prefer predictable recurring costs. Subscription aligns with ongoing content delivery, AI pet updates, and VR platform maintenance.

pricing_suggestion.justification: Mid-to-low range of competitive benchmark ($29.95-$250/mo) reflecting medium pain intensity from Problem department. Priced above MyndVR consumer ($29.95/mo) but well below ElliQ ($59/mo) and care.coach ($150-250/mo) to compete on accessibility while acknowledging free alternative (Alcove).

unit_economics.churn_source: B2B SaaS SMB monthly churn benchmark 3-5% (Optifai 2025, WeAreFounders 2026); used lower bound 3% reflecting institutional multi-year contracts per MyndVR precedent
unit_economics.margin_source: Hardware-enabled SaaS gross margin 50-65% (CloudZero 2025, Benchmarkit 2025); mid-range 55% reflecting VR headset hardware costs + content delivery infrastructure
unit_economics.cac_source: Eldercare technology CAC benchmark ~$1000 (FinancialModelsLab 2025); adjusted to $800 reflecting B2B SaaS average $702 (Optifai 2025) and relationship-driven senior living sales channel (conferences, physician referrals)

cac_plus_20.assessment: CAC increase to $960 further deteriorates already-negative unit economics, making the business model entirely unviable.

churn_plus_20.assessment: Higher churn at 3.6% monthly reduces LTV to $596, deepening the LTV/CAC deficit below 1.0x.

price_minus_20.assessment: Price reduction to $31.20/mo compresses already-thin margins, pushing payback to nearly 4 years with sub-1.0 LTV/CAC.

model_precedents.segment: Senior living facilities (VR platform for elderly)
model_precedents.evidence: $3.74M funded, 500+ senior living communities, 95% retention rate, acquired Alcove from AARP Jan 2023, NIH/NIA research grants

model_precedents.segment: Senior living facilities and home consumers (VR for elderly)
model_precedents.evidence: $2.17M funded via HTC Vive X accelerator, $600K+ contracted revenue by 2020, multi-year B2B contracts $2K-$175K/year
$249 hardware + $59/mo subscription; government-subsidized distribution in NY, FL, WA; validates willingness to pay for digital elderly companionship
$35M Series B funding for AI senior living technology; integrates care planning, safety, and staff efficiency

assumptions: ARPU $39/mo based on low-to-mid competitive range for medium pain intensity (competitive pricing benchmark)
Monthly churn 3% from B2B SaaS SMB benchmarks — lower bound reflecting institutional contracts (industry benchmark)
Gross margin 55% for hardware-enabled SaaS model with VR headset costs (industry benchmark CloudZero/Benchmarkit 2025)
CAC $800 blending eldercare tech benchmark ($1000) with B2B SaaS average ($702), assuming conference/referral-driven sales (estimate)
Subscription model assumes B2B institutional sales to senior living facilities, not direct-to-consumer
Free tier competitor (Alcove) constrains pricing floor
No freemium conversion adjustment applied — model assumes 100% paid subscribers at institutional level
